name: analyze_objective
description: Analyzes an objective and designs a complete skill as a DAG of connected tasks.

inputs.name: objective_description
inputs.description: Description of what the skill should accomplish

inputs.name: existing_tasks
inputs.description: Existing task definitions that could be reused

inputs.name: user_preferences
inputs.description: Persistent user preferences, instructions, and constraints.

outputs.name: task_definitions
outputs.description: List of task specs to create

prompt_template:
USER PREFERENCES & CONSTRAINTS: {{ user_preferences | default({}, true) | tojson }}

Design a complete skill (a DAG of tasks) to accomplish this objective:

OBJECTIVE: {{ objective_description | default('unspecified', true) }}

RULES:
7. If USER PREFERENCES mention a specific API or constraint, you MUST STRICTLY use it instead of defaulting to other APIs! Design tasks around the specified API.
1. Create 2-4 tasks connected by edges forming a DAG.
2. For HTTP API calls, use task_type "python" with COMPLETE code using urllib.request.
   - The code MUST define: def execute(inputs) and end with: outputs = execute(inputs)
   - Use ONLY standard library (urllib.request, json, os, re). NO third-party packages.
   - ALWAYS EXTRACT PARAMETERS DYNAMICALLY from `inputs.get('user_input', '')` or `inputs.get('entities', [])`.
   - NEVER, EVER hardcode a specific entity (like a city name or stock ticker) into the python code! The skill must be reusable!
   - Always handle errors with try/except.
   - Use a 15-second timeout for HTTP requests.
3. For text interpretation or formatting, use task_type "llm" (no code needed).
4. Connect tasks with edges so outputs flow to the next task's inputs.
5. Every task MUST have a descriptive name.
6. Output key names in edges must match EXACTLY between source output and target input.

FULL REUSABLE EXAMPLE — "Fetch weather for the requested location":
{
  "skill_name": "GetWeatherReport",
  "skill_description": "Fetches current weather for a dynamically extracted location and formats a readable report",
  "tags": ["weather", "api"],
  "task_definitions": [
    {
      "name": "fetch_weather",
      "description": "Fetch current weather data from wttr.in API for a dynamic location",
      "task_type": "python",
      "risk_level": "medium",
      "inputs": [{"name": "user_input", "io_type": "string", "description": "The user query containing a location"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "weather_data", "io_type": "dict", "description": "Raw weather data from API"}],
      "code": "import urllib.request\nimport urllib.parse\nimport json\nimport re\n\ndef execute(inputs):\n    user_input = inputs.get('user_input', '')\n    # Extract location dynamically — DO NOT HARDCODE!\n    location = re.search(r'\\b\\d{5}\\b', user_input)\n    if location:\n        loc_str = location.group()\n    else:\n        words = user_input.replace(',', '').replace('?', '').split()\n        # E.g. find word after 'in' or 'for'\n        try:\n            idx = next(i for i, w in enumerate(words) if w.lower() in ('in', 'for'))\n            loc_str = ' '.join(words[idx+1:])\n        except StopIteration:\n            loc_str = words[-1] if words else 'Seattle'\n    try:\n        loc_encoded = urllib.parse.quote(loc_str)\n        url = f'https://wttr.in/{loc_encoded}?format=j1'\n        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15) as resp:\n            data = json.loads(resp.read().decode())\n        current = data.get('current_condition', [{}])[0]\n        return {\n            'weather_data': {\n                'location': loc_str,\n                'temp_f': current.get('temp_F', 'N/A'),\n                'temp_c': current.get('temp_C', 'N/A'),\n                'condition': current.get('weatherDesc', [{}])[0].get('value', 'N/A'),\n                'humidity': current.get('humidity', 'N/A'),\n                'wind_mph': current.get('windspeedMiles', 'N/A')\n            }\n        }\n    except Exception as e:\n        return {'weather_data': {'error': str(e), 'location': loc_str}}\noutputs = execute(inputs)"
    },
    {
      "name": "format_report",
      "description": "Format weather data into a human-readable report",
      "task_type": "llm",
      "risk_level": "low",
      "inputs": [{"name": "weather_data", "io_type": "dict", "description": "Weather data from API"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "report", "io_type": "string", "description": "Human-readable weather report"}]
    }
  ],
  "edges": [
    {
      "source_task_name": "fetch_weather",
      "target_task_name": "format_report",
      "output_mapping": {"weather_data": "weather_data"},
      "data_policy": "pass_through"
    }
  ]
}

Now design a reusable skill for this objective. Respond with ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "skill_name": "...",
  "skill_description": "...",
  "tags": ["..."],
  "task_definitions": [
    {
      "name": "descriptive_name",
      "description": "...",
      "task_type": "python|llm",
      "risk_level": "low|medium",
      "inputs": [{"name":"...","io_type":"string|int|float|bool|list|dict|any","description":"..."}],
      "outputs": [{"name":"...","io_type":"string|int|float|bool|list|dict|any","description":"..."}],
      "code": "REQUIRED for python tasks — full executable code with execute(inputs). YOU MUST NOT HARDCODE SPECIFIC VALUES (use dynamic extraction from inputs)."
    }
  ],
  "edges": [
    {
      "source_task_name": "...",
      "target_task_name": "...",
      "output_mapping": {"source_output_key": "target_input_key"},
      "data_policy": "pass_through"
    }
  ]
}
system_instruction: You are a skill architect. Design reusable task DAGs with NO HARDCODED SPECIFIC VALUES. Always extract entities (locations, symbols) dynamically from the inputs list or user_input string. Always respond with valid JSON only. For HTTP/API tasks, ALWAYS use task_type python with complete executable code using urllib.request.
